text: When designing REST APIs, it is important to use clear and consistent endpoint names. For example, use `/users` instead of `/getUsers` to keep URLs simple and logical. Always include appropriate HTTP status codes in your responses, such as 200 for success, 201 when a resource is created, 404 if something is not found, and 500 for server errors. Good documentation is essential; it helps developers understand how to use your API correctly. You should also plan for versioning from the start. Adding a version number like `/v1/` to your endpoints allows you to make changes in the future without breaking existing integrations.
